title: The Wildest Sun A Novel
author: Asha Lemmie
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Wildest Sun A Novel" book by Asha Lemmie?
Options:
A. We dance until dawn, stopping only so I can gulp down my non-alcoholic mojito in three eager mouthfuls. They really are tasty; I adore the mint. I'm drenched in sweat, and I know my curls have fallen and my stringy hair is plastered to my forehead. He scoops me up in his arms and spins me around to the beat when my feet finally collapse under me. He is fearless. The music brings him to life; nothing can stop him. On this island, with the music pulsing through my veins, I feel the weight of everything that's happened melt away, if only briefly. I'm so light that I swear I could soar like one of the seagulls that ride the wind, hovering between sea and sky.
B. We dance the whole night, only stopping so I can chug my non-alcoholic mojito in three eager gulps. They really are tasty; I adore the mint. I'm drenched in sweat, and I know my curls have fallen and my stringy hair is sticking to my forehead. He lifts me up in his arms and spins me around to the rhythm when my feet eventually fail me. He is fearless. The music brings him to life; nothing can stop him. On this island, with the music flowing through my veins, I feel the weight of everything that's happened fade away, if only for now. I'm so light that I swear I could fly like one of the seagulls that ride the wind, hovering between sea and sky.
C. We dance all night, stopping only for me to down my liquor-free mojito in three desperate gulps. They really are delicious; I love the mint. I am drenched in sweat, and I know that my curls have fallen and my stringy hair is clinging to my forehead. He picks me up bridal style and whirls me around to the beat when my feet  finally give out on me. He is dauntless. The music makes him come alive; nothing can stop him. On this island, with the music running through my veins, I feel the weight of all that has happened slide away, if just for now. I am so light that I swear I could take flight like one of the gulls that ride the wind, hovering between water and sky. 
D. We boogie all night, pausing only for me to down my liquor-free mojito in three desperate swigs. They truly are delicious; I love the mint. I am soaked in sweat, and I know my curls have dropped and my stringy hair is clinging to my forehead. He picks me up bride-style and whirls me around to the beat when my feet finally give out on me. He is dauntless. The music makes him come alive; nothing can stop him. On this island, with the music running through my veins, I feel the burden of all that has transpired slide away, if just temporarily. I am so light that I swear I could take flight like one of the seagulls that ride the wind, hovering between ocean and sky.
Answer:

I'm drenched in sweat, and I know my curls have fallen and my stringy hair is plastered to my forehead. He scoops me up in his arms and spins me around to the beat when my feet finally collapse under me. He is fearless. The music brings him to life; nothing can stop him. On this island, with the music pulsing through my veins, I feel the weight of everything that's happened melt away, if only briefly. I'm so light that I swear I could soar like one of the seagulls that ride the wind, hovering between sea and sky.
